title: Tinus lepidota
taxonomy: Tinus lepidota is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Buxaceae. It is native to China and is also known as the 'Frilly Boxwood' due to its distinctive foliage. This evergreen shrub is often used in horticulture for its attractive and compact growth habit. Tinus lepidota is also sometimes referred to as Pachysandra lepidota, although it is widely recognized as a synonym of the genus Tinus. The species is valued for its ornamental appeal and ability to thrive in shaded environments.
